data.title: 3 'Strong Buy' Stocks to Buy Now, 1/26/2026, According to Top Analysts
data.description:
Looking for top-rated stocks to buy now? Recent analyst updates highlight three companies that have earned Strong Buy ratings from Top Wall Street analysts. Each stock offers solid upside potential, supported by fresh Buy calls.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For more stock ideas, check out TipRanks' Analyst Top Stocks tool, which tracks the latest Buy ratings from top analysts in real time.

Here are today's top analyst-backed stock picks. Click on any ticker to research the company before deciding whether to add it to your portfolio.

Exagen (XGN) – Exagen develops blood-based diagnostic tests for autoimmune diseases such as lupus and rheumatoid arthritis. Today, Canaccord Genuity analyst Kyle Mikson maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a price target of $15 per share. In the last three months, all four Top Analysts covering the stock have rated it a Buy. Taken together, their 12-month price targets imply an upside of about 204.35%.

Vertical Aerospace (EVTL) – Vertical Aerospace designs and develops electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft for urban air mobility and short-haul air travel. Today, William Blair analyst Louie DiPalma maintained a Buy rating on the stock. In the last three months, all three Top Analysts covering the stock have rated it a Buy. Taken together, their 12-month price targets imply an upside of about 96.43%.

Par Technology (PAR) – PAR Technology provides cloud-based software and hardware solutions for restaurants and hospitality businesses. Today, BTIG analyst Andrew Harte maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a price target of $60 per share. In the last three months, all four Top Analysts covering the stock have rated it a Buy. Taken together, their 12-month price targets imply an upside of about 101.74%.

Who Are the Top Analysts? 

TipRanks ranks financial analysts according to the success rates of their ratings and the average return on each of their ratings. The Top Analysts have each earned a five-star ranking, thanks to the accuracy and profitability of their ratings over time. 

See real-time analyst rankings and learn more about the performance of Top Analysts on TipRanks' Top Wall Street Analysts page.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Zoom Stock (ZM) Jumps 10% as Investment in Anthropic Estimated at $4 Billion
data.description:
Shares of Zoom Communications (ZM) are up 10% on Jan. 26 after analysts at Baird estimated the company's investment in artificial intelligence startup Anthropic could be worth as much as $4 billion.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In May 2023, Anthropic announced a partnership with Zoom and revealed that Zoom Ventures had invested in the company. The companies did not disclose the value of the investment at that time, but Zoom reported making $51 million in "strategic investments" that quarter.

Baird's analysts now estimate that investment went entirely to Anthropic. The startup is currently valued at $350 billion, suggesting that Zoom's stake could see a return of roughly 78 times its initial investment, according to the analysts at Baird. Those estimates put the current value of Zoom's investment in Anthropic at $2 billion to $4 billion, depending on dilution assumptions.

Anthropic's Rapid Growth

"While we (and the market) have been primarily focused on ZM's ability to gradually reaccelerate revenue growth and capture AI opportunities, the quieter, hidden gem might be its $51 million investment in Anthropic in 2023," wrote Baird in a note to clients.

Zoom, a video conference company, was popular during the Covid-19 pandemic. But its fortunes and share price have since faded. In contrast, privately held Anthropic has seen its growth and valuation explode over the past 18 months as the AI trade has accelerated. As such, Zoom's investment in Anthropic could prove to be a huge long-term benefit, writes Baird.

Is ZM Stock a Buy?

Zoom's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 21 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 13 Buy, seven Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average ZM price target of $99.11 implies 4.78% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Analysts Like Nvidia's (NVDA) Latest $2B Investment in CoreWeave
data.description:
Wall Street has started to cheer chipmaker Nvidia's (NVDA) ai-factory-buildout">new $2 billion deal with AI cloud provider CoreWeave (CRWV). While Raymond James called it a "vote of confidence" in CoreWeave's AI cloud capacity, Needham sees future credit guarantees from Nvidia for CoreWeave's future leases for high-performance computing (HPC).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As part of the deal, CoreWeave is tasked with developing over 5 gigawatts of power capacity by 2030 by developing and operating AI factories using Nvidia's infrastructure, such as the Rubin platform, the Vera CPUs, and the Bluefield technology for storage systems. In return, Nvidia has agreed to buy Class A common shares in CoreWeave at $87.20 per share.

Needham Hammers Credit Guarantees for CoreWeave

The deal comes at a time when CoreWeave is battling pressure from investors despite topping Wall Street estimates in its Q3 2025 earnings results. Investors have dragged the Neocloud company to court over project delays in its AI infrastructure buildout, despite a recent update on the removal of the delay identified earlier.

Commenting, Needham emphasized how securing future leases from HPC operators had become difficult for CoreWeave due to concerns about its financial base and competition for hyperscaler deals, and how the deal could change that. CoreWeave builds and owns its data centers, but also leases additional sites from HPC operators.

"On the [sell-side analyst] call back [with CoreWeave], it became clear that NVIDIA would look to offer a backstop or credit guarantee on future leases to make CoreWeave more competitive with investment-grade hyperscalers," Needham clarified.

Davidson Sees Fewer Risks for CoreWeave in 2026

Chipping in, DA Davidson analyst Alex Platt noted that CoreWeave continues to enjoy strong demand for its computing capacity, with the deal helping to ease concerns about CoreWeave's vulnerability to third-party risks that have factored into its shares. In addition, Platt sees "multiple de-risking pathways" in 2026 for the New Jersey-based company.

He raised his CRWV price target by 62% from $68 per share to $110 and elevated the shares from Hold to Buy.

Raymond James Flags Earnings Potential for Nvidia

Meanwhile, Raymond James estimates Nvidia could earn between $60–$90 billion from selling systems and networking to CoreWeave between 2026 and 2030, representing about 40% of revenue from the deal, plus $9–$13 billion in graphics processing unit sales per gigawatt.

In addition, the financial services firm contended that Nvidia has a strong strategic reason to cultivate an ecosystem of independent customers and partners that rely on its chips to make its business less vulnerable to big cloud providers or hyperscalers that are building and using their own in-house AI accelerators.

Is CRWV Stock a Good Buy?

Across Wall Street, CoreWeave's shares remain a Moderate Buy based on analysts' consensus rating. This breaks down to 13 Buys and 12 Holds issued over the past three months.

However, the average CRWV price target of $123.86 implies about 22% growth potential.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Trump Considers Easing Federal Agent Presence in Minnesota
data.description:
Following mass public outcry over the shooting of 37-year-old Alex Pretti in Minneapolis over the weekend, President Trump is weighing whether to reduce the number of federal agents in the state if local officials work more closely with federal authorities.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The development comes after Trump spoke with Minnesota Governor Tim Walz over the phone on Monday, with both sides describing the call positively. "It was a very good call, and we, actually, seemed to be on a similar wavelength," Trump said in a Truth Social post.

Trump Sends 'Border Czar' to Minnesota

Earlier in the day, Trump said he would send "Border Czar" Tom Homan to Minnesota while announcing an investigation into welfare fraud within the state. An Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) veteran, Homan is seen favoring a more targeted enforcement approach rather than a broad strategy.

Meanwhile, Trump declined to take a stance on the shooting of Pretti, contrasting with other administration officials who said Pretti was at fault for bringing a gun to the protest. The case is currently being investigated by the FBI and the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP).

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Silver Prices Face Turbulence': Goldman Sachs Sees More Wild Swings Ahead
data.description:
Silver (SLV) prices are holding firm above the psychologically important $100-per-ounce mark today, with spot levels hovering in the low-$100s as traders digest another leg of the metal's remarkable run. The move caps off an extraordinary period for silver, which surged 138% last year and has remained elevated in recent sessions.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Part of what makes silver's rally so dramatic is not just demand, but the way the market is structured. In a recent research note, Goldman Sachs' commodities strategists argued that silver's extreme price swings are being amplified by unusually tight inventories in key trading hubs, particularly London, where much of the world's benchmark pricing is set.

"Extreme price action is likely to persist," the bank warned, pointing to thin available supply and reduced market liquidity as key drivers of volatility.

Goldman explained that concerns over potential trade restrictions and tariffs last year triggered a rush to move physical silver into the United States, draining stocks from London vaults and distorting the global flow of metal. That shift has made prices far more sensitive to incremental buying and selling. According to the bank, levels of weekly demand that would historically move prices by about 2% are now capable of driving swings closer to 7%, simply because there is less readily available metal to absorb those flows.

Crucially, Goldman does not see the rally as proof of a fundamental shortage of silver. Instead, the strategists describe the market as suffering from a "location problem" rather than a production problem – silver exists, but not always where the trading system needs it most. Until inventories are rebuilt and liquidity improves, the bank believes sharp rallies and pullbacks are likely to remain a defining feature of the market.

Recent developments appear to support that view. Ongoing geopolitical tensions have kept safe-haven demand alive, while expectations for lower U.S. interest rates have increased the appeal of precious metals more broadly. At the same time, policy changes affecting metal exports and physical supply chains continue to inject uncertainty into the market, reinforcing the conditions Goldman says can exaggerate price moves.

Is Silver Worth Buying Right Now?

A look at TipRanks' technical analysis dashboard suggests silver is tilting bullish overall. In the one-day summary, 15 indicators point to a Buy signal, compared with just one Neutral and six Bearish readings. The strength is coming primarily from moving averages, which flash a Strong Buy with 12 Bullish signals and none Bearish, even as oscillators lean the other way with a Sell reading.
data.author: Ben Yoffe

data.title: Why Investors May Get Paid Sooner from Autonomous Cars than Humanoid Robots
data.description:
Autonomous vehicles have a clearer near-term investing edge than humanoid robots, according to tech venture capitalist Bill Gurley. Speaking on Yahoo Finance's Opening Bid Unfiltered, Gurley said that self-driving cars are further along because the problems they solve are more predictable and defined. In contrast, humanoid robots are still in an early phase, where the industry has not yet agreed on what they should be best used for or how they should work at scale.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Gurley also explained that most humanoid robot projects today are highly customized for specific warehouses or environments, making it hard to create a single standard product that works well everywhere. As a result, scaling humanoid robots into a large, profitable business is much more difficult right now. It's worth noting that Gurley's views carry weight given his long career, which includes early investments in Uber (UBER), Twitter (now X), and Nextdoor (NXDR).

Still, Gurley emphasized that both autonomous cars and humanoid robots are challenging businesses because they require heavy hardware investment and large amounts of capital. Nevertheless, this hasn't stopped Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk from pursuing both opportunities at the same time, although Musk has also admitted that early production will be slow. In addition, a recent Gartner report found that by 2028, fewer than 20 companies are expected to move humanoid robots into full production. This shows just how early the technology is.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 10 Buys, eight Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $398.38 per share implies 8.5% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) to Sign Memory Chip Deal with Samsung
data.description:
Multiple media reports say that Nvidia (NVDA) is planning to source memory chips from Samsung Electronics (SSNLF).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Under terms of the deal, Samsung will supply Nvidia with next-generation memory chips. Samsung is reportedly ready to begin producing high-bandwidth memory chips, called "HBM4," as early as February of this year and start shipping them to Nvidia.

Samsung is a global leader when it comes to making HBM memory microchips, which are critically important for the latest artificial-intelligence (AI) processors. Surging demand for HBM chips has been a big driver of Samsung's sales in recent quarters.

Impacts on Micron Stock (MU)

News of Samsung's contract win has shares of rival Micron Technology (MU) down about 3%. Micron is the chief rival of Samsung when it comes to the memory chip market. However, Samsung's progress in meeting requirements for Nvidia doesn't mean a loss of sales for Micron, with mega-cap technology companies racing to buy as many HBM memory chips as they can.

In fact, Micron's supply of HBM memory chips for 2026 is sold out. Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra told analysts on the company's most recent earnings call that the company is ramping up its HBM memory chip production to meet future demand.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidia's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 42 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 40 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the past three months. The average NVDA price target of $263.11 implies 41.10% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: The Wall Street Slayer on Nvidia (NVDA), CoreWeave (CRWV), and the Silicon Ouroboros of AI Hype
data.description:
There's something almost terrifying about how the AI boom has turned circular logic into a multi-trillion-dollar circus. Take CoreWeave (CRWV). On Monday, shares of the crypto-bros-turned-AI-hustlers jumped 6% after Nvidia (NVDA) announced it was throwing another $2 billion at the company. The "investment" is supposed to help build over 5 gigawatts of AI "factory" capacity by 2030.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Investors applauded like they'd just witnessed Elon landing on Mars without the ability to get back to Earth rather than noticing the obvious: a supplier is funding a customer so the customer can buy more from the supplier. This, investors, is what passes for momentum these days.

Nvidia sells the chips. Nvidia funds the infrastructure company that buys the chips. The infrastructure company borrows against the chips to buy more chips. The market rewards everyone involved for "execution." Somewhere in the middle of this loop, someone drops the phrase "AI native software," Wall Street applauds as if it's Pulitzer-worthy, and the share price levitates another few percent.

At the center, of course, is Jensen Huang, Nvidia's leather-jacketed high priest, giving keynote speeches like Moses descending from Mount CUDA. Every sentence drips destiny: AI factories. New industrial revolutions. Gazillion-dollar opportunities. And yes, Nvidia has built something remarkable. But right now it feels less like innovation and more like Huang quietly realizing the best customer for Nvidia… is Nvidia itself.

And then there's CoreWeave, now hailed as a pillar of next-generation AI infrastructure, despite starting life as a crypto miner called Atlantic Crypto. Really. When Bitcoin stopped paying for Lambos, they pivoted to renting GPUs to anyone willing to say "large language model" without laughing. Although with the Trumps now in crypto – American Bitcoin, Melania coin, yes we made it cypherpunks! – the grift just went up a notch or a thousand and this pivot feels less like foresight and more like self-preservation. If the original crypto miners are running to less corrupt corners, can you really blame them? Analysts applaud this as visionary adaptability, but it's the same reckless greed in a slightly smarter suit.

Look, The Slayer isn't saying AI demand is fake. There is real demand. It's that so much growth is being propped up by companies investing in each other to keep the story tidy. Nvidia owns a chunk of CoreWeave. CoreWeave depends on Nvidia hardware. Both profit when the market pretends this counts as organic expansion rather than balance-sheet gymnastics.

And the market doesn't just tolerate it – it salivates over it. Circular deals keep the wheel spinning while giving everyone the illusion of control. They avoid awkward questions about energy costs, utilization rates, or what happens when "build it and they will come" turns into "we built it and now we're funding ourselves to pretend they arrived."

So, the snake keeps eating its tail and the valuations edge higher. But somewhere in the background, reality is waiting, like it always does, for the music to stop.
data.author: Wall Street Slayer
